data.title: Société BIC SA reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Société BIC SA press release (OTCPK:BICEY): Q3 net sales of €540M, up 0.2%.
  • Adjusted EPS of €1.77, up 19% versus Q3 2023 (€1.49).
  • Q3 Adjusted EBIT of €102M.
  • Full Year 2024 Net Sales are expected to grow low single digit at constant currency.

data.title: ServiceNow in charts: Subscription revenue continues to grow in double-digits in Q3
Osisko Mining to be removed from S&P/TSX Composite Index
data.description:
• Osisko Mining (TSX:OSK:CA) shares will be removed from the S&P/TSX Composite Index before the open of trading on Monday, October 28, 2024.
  • The shareholders of Osisko Mining (OSK:CA) have agreed to the terms of a Plan of Arrangement resulting in the acquisition by Gold Fields. Each share of Osisko Mining will receive $4.90 cash consideration.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Annaly Capital in charts: Net servicing income continues to improve; economic return stands at 4.9% in Q3
Whirlpool beats Q3 profit estimates, sets full year outlook above street view
data.description:
Whirlpool (NYSE:WHR) shares were getting a bump in after-hours trading as the company earned a better-than-expected Q3 profit even as sales in the third quarter fell 19% to a below consensus $4B.

“In Q3 we continued to deliver sequential ongoing EBIT margin expansion despite the unfavorable macroeconomic environment we are experiencing in North America,” said CEO Marc Bitzer, adding that the company remains “well positioned to benefit from the eventual U.S. housing market recovery.”

Regionally, sales of major domestic appliances were softest in North America, down 4.3% year-over-year, compared to a 9.1% gain in Asia and 0.4% gain in Latin America. The sales of small domestic appliances dropped 3% globally.

The Benton Harbor, Michigan company reaffirmed its FY24 adjusted EPS guidance of $12.00 which is higher than the consensus estimates of $11.51 per share. Net sales are reaffirmed at $16.9B, down from $19.45B in 2023 but slightly better than the estimated $16.78B.

Whirlpool (WHR) shares were higher by 3.6% in postmarket after trading in the red for three consecutive days.

data.title: Small-caps go 739 trading days without a new record high, but Jefferies sees more upside
data.description:
One of the notable themes of the U.S. equity market this year has been the rotation out of megacap technology stocks and into other corners of the market, with one of those avenues being small-caps.

Wall Street's benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) is a whopping +21.54% YTD, as a bull run that started a little over two years ago continues to chug along. In comparison, the Russell 2000 gauge (RTY) - the barometer for small-cap stocks - is a much less impressive +9.00%.

However, the YTD performance doesn't really paint a true picture of the situation. The rotation out of megacap technology stocks began in earnest in July, triggered by that month's consumer inflation report which showed the headline consumer price index (CPI) falling on a M/M basis for the first time since December 2022.

That June CPI report gave investors the confidence that inflation was finally largely under control and spurred them to start moving out of technology stocks and into other assets, including small-caps.

Since that CPI report on July 11, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) and the Russell 2000 (RTY) have advanced nearly the same amount. The former is +3.81% while the latter is +3.78%.

The RTY in fact hit its 52-week high of 2,302.80 points soon after that CPI report, on July 31. The small-cap barometer is still 11.28% away from its all-time intraday high of 2,458.86 notched on November 8, 2021, 739 trading days ago.

But Jefferies believes the RTY still has more upside in 2024. The brokerage has raised its year-end target for the Russell 2000 (RTY) to 2,420 points.

"Our year-end target seemed too low to us based on our more optimistic viewpoint. Thus, we raise our Russell 2000 target to 2420, 8% upside from here. We think Fed cuts along with the feeling that we could see a rally similar to '16, drives us to our new target. Based on consensus earnings, this would put the RUT's P/E at 23.1x," Steven DeSanctis, equity strategist at Jefferies, said in a note on Monday.

"We think macro backdrop supports case for small caps: We believe the biggest macro driver is that HY spreads are at their tightest since '21 and well below their long-term average. Company balance sheets are in very good shape with near record levels of cash, and capital markets are open. We have seen the shape of the curve finally turn positive, albeit less than 20bps, which also bodes well for relative performance," DeSanctis added.

Earlier this month, Morgan Stanley upgraded small-caps to Neutral versus large-caps, citing the blowout September jobs report and the Fed's large half-point rate cut. The brokerage said that it was still looking for macro indicators to show an oncoming "clear acceleration in growth" to turn outright bullish on small-caps overall.

The Russell 2000 index was launched on January 1, 1984, and is currently maintained by the London Stock Exchange's (OTCPK:LDNXF)(OTCPK:LNSTY) FTSE Russell unit. The average market cap of the companies listed on the gauge is $3.55B as of September end.

For investors looking to track small-cap stocks, here are some exchange-traded funds of interest: (IJR), (IWM), (VB), (VBR), (VBK), (SCHA), (SPSM), and (DFAS).

More on small-caps

  • Are Reports Of Small-Cap Stocks' Revival Prospects Premature?
  • Dancing In September, A Fed Cut To Remember
  • Russell 2000: Soft To No-Landing Is Supporting Another Bullish Upleg
  • Top small-cap stock picks from Morgan Stanley following the group's upgrade to neutral
  • Dow notches record close, S&P rises as small-cap rally, rotation helps Wall Street rebound

data.title: Offshore drillers Transocean, Seadrill discussing merger - Bloomberg
data.description:
Seadrill (NYSE:SDRL) +10.2% post-market Wednesday following a Bloomberg report it is in ongoing discussions with larger offshore drilling contractor Transocean (NYSE:RIG) about a potential merger.

Merging with Seadrill (SDRL) would fit Transocean's (RIG) strategy of growing its list of backlog work, utilizing some of the world's most advanced rigs capable of drilling in water more than two miles deep, according to the report.

Once the world's two biggest offshore drillers, both companies have lagged amid a protracted downturn in the industry that forced many contractors into bankruptcy; offshore drilling, particularly deepwater, is again booming due to steady oil prices and slower production growth from U.S. shale fields.

Transocean's (RIG) stock price has slumped 35% so far this year, giving the company a market value of $3.6B, and Seadrill (SDRL) shares are down 26% YTD, giving it a market cap of $2.4B.

data.title: Hanmi Financial upped to Overweight as Piper sees 'meaningful' NIM expansion
data.description:
Piper Sandler initiated coverage on Hanmi Financial Corp. (NASDAQ:HAFC) stock with an Overweight rating on Wednesday as the bank's Q3 results suggest a "meaningful" expansion in net interest margin going forward.

During the quarter ended Sept. 30, Hanmi's (HAFC) NIM rose five basis points to 2.74%, driven by lower funding costs and higher yields on interest-earning assets. Strong operational performance sent net interest income 2.9% higher from Q2. Loan and deposit growth also came in solid.

“Hanmi is well-positioned for a strong close to 2024 with a robust balance sheet, ample liquidity, healthy capital ratios, and a solid loan pipeline," Bonnie Lee, the company's president and CEO, had said in a statement.

In all, "a much better NIM outlook and core deposit funded loan growth traction" stoked the analyst upgrade.

Piper's Overweight rating diverges from the SA Quant system rating and the average Wall Street analyst rating, both at Hold.
